reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully encodes the informal claim. `ZMod (p^m)` is $\mathbb{Z}/p^m\mathbb{Z}$, and `span ({↑p} : Set (ZMod (p^m)))` is exactly the ideal generated by the image of $p$, i.e., $p\mathbb{Z}/p^m\mathbb{Z}$. Mathlib's `IsNilpotent` on an ideal $I$ states $\exists n, I^n = \bot$, which matches the informal definition (the $n \geq 1$ restriction is automatic since $I^n = 0 \Rightarrow I^{n+1} = 0$). The primality hypothesis is retained (as `_hp`), matching the informal assumption. No hypotheses are silently added or dropped and no types are changed.
